summary: Replaced asm/MSL/cstring.o with a new C implementation at src/MSL/cstring.c, adding prototypes for cstring and mem_funcs helpers and extending stddef.h with wchar_t. The decompiled file implements memcmp, memchr, memmove, and an uncertain widechar-to-byte conversion routine left as func_8032388C because the author was not sure of the official symbol name.
what_changed: Added src/MSL/cstring.c with C implementations for func_8032388C, memcmp, memchr, and memmove.
Added include/cstring.h declaring func_8032388C, memcmp, memchr, and memmove.
Added include/mem_funcs.h declaring external copy helper routines: __copy_longs_aligned, __copy_longs_rev_aligned, __copy_longs_unaligned, and __copy_longs_rev_unaligned.
Updated include/stddef.h by normalizing the size_t typedef whitespace and adding typedef unsigned short wchar_t.
Updated obj_files.mk to build $(BUILD_DIR)/src/MSL/cstring.o instead of $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/MSL/cstring.o.
Left asm/MSL/mem_funcs.o in the build while calling its helper routines from the newly decompiled cstring.c.
smart_moves: Kept the uncertain routine name as func_8032388C rather than guessing an official symbol; the PR body explicitly notes uncertainty about its name.
Factored declarations for the still-asm mem_funcs helpers into include/mem_funcs.h, allowing cstring.c to call the existing optimized copy routines without decompiling them in the same PR.
Implemented memmove with a size threshold and alignment/overlap dispatch to the four long-copy helpers, preserving the original library-style fast path structure.
Used pointer pre-decrement plus pre-increment loop forms in memcmp, memchr, and forward small-copy memmove paths, a common matching tactic for Metrowerks-generated loops.
Added wchar_t to stddef.h only when needed by the widechar-related routine, keeping the header change minimal.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling an MSL library object, swap only the completed object from asm to src in obj_files.mk and leave dependent asm helper objects in place until they are separately matched.
For standard-library routines, matching may require preserving compiler idioms such as len++ followed by while (--len > 0), pointer initialization to ptr - 1, and pre-increment loads.
memmove matching should account for both overlap direction and alignment class; this PR splits small byte-copy loops from large copies delegated to aligned or unaligned long-copy helper routines.
Uncertain library symbols should be documented in comments or PR text and left with their address-style placeholder name until stronger evidence appears.
Header work can be part of decomp matching: adding correct prototypes and primitive typedefs such as wchar_t may be necessary before newly decompiled library code can compile cleanly.
naming_conventions: Unknown or unconfirmed functions use address-derived names such as func_8032388C.
Internal MSL copy helpers are declared with double-underscore names: __copy_longs_aligned, __copy_longs_rev_aligned, __copy_longs_unaligned, and __copy_longs_rev_unaligned.
MSL C standard routines keep canonical lowercase names such as memcmp, memchr, and memmove.
Header guards follow double-underscore all-caps forms such as __CSTRING_H__, __MEM_FUNCS_H__, and __STDDEF_H__.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced asm/MSL/cstring.o with src/MSL/cstring.o in obj_files.mk as the build integration step.
Used u8 pointer casts for byte-wise memory comparisons, searches, and copies.
Used len++ and pre-increment/pre-decrement pointer loops to mirror likely emitted assembly control flow.
For memmove, computed copy direction with int reverse = (u32)src < (u32)dst.
For len >= 32, dispatched to long-copy helper routines based on whether destination and source alignments differ via (((u32)dst ^ (u32)src) & 3) and whether copy direction is reverse.
For len < 32, performed explicit byte-copy loops forward from ptr - 1 or backward from ptr + len.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only explicit author note was uncertainty about the official name for func_8032388C.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs or commits that rename func_8032388C to an official MSL symbol.
Find PRs that decompile asm/MSL/mem_funcs.o or define the __copy_longs_* helper bodies.
Search for later changes to include/cstring.h or stddef.h involving wchar_t or cstring prototypes.
Compare later memmove implementations or fixes for overlap detection and alignment handling.
